seoTitle: How to Add a Checkout to a Canva Website
metaDescription: Learn how to set up a checkout on your Canva website, connecting payment options so customers can buy directly from your site.
intro: Adding a checkout to a Canva website lets small businesses and freelancers sell products or services directly, without building a separate e-commerce site. This guide covers setting up that purchasing flow.

steps.heading: Open your Canva website project
steps.body: Go into the website builder for the site you want to add checkout functionality to.

steps.heading: Find the checkout or payment integration section
steps.body: Look in the site's settings or elements panel for payment button or checkout integration options.

steps.heading: Connect a payment provider
steps.body: Link a supported payment method so customers can pay securely when they check out.

steps.heading: Add checkout buttons to relevant pages
steps.body: Place a 'Buy Now' or 'Checkout' button on your product or service pages so it's easy for visitors to purchase.

steps.heading: Test the checkout flow
steps.body: Walk through the purchase process yourself to confirm everything works correctly before publishing.

faq.question: What payment methods can I connect to a Canva website checkout?
faq.answer: Canva supports connecting common payment integrations; check your site's settings for the currently available providers.

faq.question: Is a Canva website checkout suitable for a full online store?
faq.answer: It works well for simple product or service sales, though larger stores may need a dedicated e-commerce platform.

faq.question: Do I need Canva Pro to use website checkout features?
faq.answer: Some website features are Pro-only, so check your plan's website capabilities before setting this up.

faq.question: Can I track sales made through my Canva website?
faq.answer: Sales tracking typically depends on the payment provider you connect, which usually provides its own dashboard.
